title: rubus hirtus f. orbiculatus
intro: Rubus hirtus f. orbiculatus, also known as the thorny bramble, is a perennial shrub native to East Asia, belonging to the rose family and characterized by its thorny stems and edible fruit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus f. orbiculatus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, making it a valuable herb for preventing and treating various diseases. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other remedies for topical and internal use.
